events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes multiple hazards on a production floor: cords across walkways, missing guardrails, blocked egress/extinguishers, improvised scaffolds, unattended open holes. Work is in-progress. This is clearly a fail with critical/high risk.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 50,

  "findings": [
    "Electrical cords across walkways create trip hazards and potential damage to cords",
    "Missing guardrails at elevated areas or platform edges exposes workers to fall hazards",
    "Blocked egress paths prevent emergency evacuation",
    "Blocked fire extinguishers delay emergency response",
    "Improvised scaffold assembly without proper engineering or component verification violates scaffold standards",
    "Unattended open holes without covers or guardrails create fall hazards"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reroute cords overhead or use cord covers rated for the traffic; remove from walkway immediately",
    "Install compliant guardrails or stop work at the edge until fall protection is in place",
    "Clear egress paths immediately and keep them unobstructed at all times",
    "Clear access to fire extinguishers and maintain minimum 36-inch clearance",
    "Remove improvised scaffold from service; have a competent person inspect and erect using manufacturer components",
    "Cover open holes with secured covers rated for twice the intended load or install guardrails; post warning signs"
  ],
  "rationale": "Multiple serious hazards are present simultaneously on an active production floor, including fall hazards, trip hazards, blocked emergency egress, and non-compliant scaffolding. These conditions violate OSHA general duty and 1926 Subpart L requirements, creating imminent danger to workers. Work must stop on affected areas until controls are implemented.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA General Duty Clause Section 5(a)(1)",
    "29 CFR 1926 Subpart L - Scaffolds",
    "29 CFR 1910.37 - Exit routes",
    "29 CFR 1910.157 - Portable fire extinguishers"
  ]
}
